commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair remains in the PR branch: classify token-query and refresh-auth remotes as external and prove it with focused tests.; Needs attention: Remote auth material can still be copied into OpenClaw-managed per-agent mcporter config for token-query or refresh-only forms.; Review finding: [P1] Keep refresh and query-token remotes external — extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts:209... (sha=43794b4e8c60f990ee346f8e9333a142469197b1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes QMD memory use per-agent mcporter config files, separates qmd and mcporter spawn environments, preserves selected configured mcporter servers, parses log-prefixed mcporter JSON, and stabilizes one agent MCP runtime test wait.

PR surface: Source +352, Tests +680. Total +1032 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows mcporter inherits QMD-scoped XDG env, and the PR body supplies real local mcporter/qmd proof; the remaining auth-classification defect is source-reproducible from the new classifier and upstream mcporter config schema.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair remains in the PR branch: classify token-query and refresh-auth remotes as external and prove it with focused tests.

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate QMD mcporter daemon/config state per agent by writing an agent-scoped mcporter config and passing it to mcporter calls.
- Preserve the configured mcporter server definition instead of replacing custom/remote server config with a hard-coded local `qmd mcp` entry.
- Keep QMD model/env settings flowing only through the generated stdio server entry, while stripping QMD-scoped XDG env from mcporter's own process environment.
- Tolerate mcporter daemon log lines before JSON responses so warm daemon calls do not break memory search parsing.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving the configured `qmd` mcporter server and avoiding QMD-scoped XDG env leakage into mcporter's own process env.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node `v24.15.0`, `mcporter 0.10.2`, `qmd 2.1.0`, configured agents `hex` and `trump`, and the configured mcporter `qmd` stdio server. Home paths are redacted as `~`; private snippets are omitted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --version
mcporter --version
qmd --version
mcporter config get qmd --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
node -e '<read generated ~/.openclaw/agents/{hex,trump}/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json and print redacted command/env summary>'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output, redacted:

```text
node --version -> v24.15.0
mcporter --version -> 0.10.2
qmd --version -> qmd 2.1.0

mcporter config get qmd --json
{
  "name": "qmd",
  "source": { "kind": "local", "path": "~/.mcporter/mcporter.json" },
  "transport": "stdio",
  "command": "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd",
  "args": ["mcp"]
}

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
  agentId=hex backend=qmd files=1310 chunks=1310 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
  agentId=trump backend=qmd files=204 chunks=204 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=memory; first.score=0.93

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=sessions; first.score=0.93

Generated mcporter config summaries:
hex:   path=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json   imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-cache
trump: path=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-cache
```

**Observed result after fix:** Both agents reported healthy QMD vector readiness (`embeddingProbe.ok=true`, `vector.available=true`, `vector.semanticAvailable=true`), both `memory search` commands returned one result successfully, and the generated mcporter configs were separate per agent while preserving the configured `qmd` stdio command.

**What was not tested:** I did not test a live remote HTTP mcporter server; that preservation path is covered by the focused unit test added in this PR.
target.author: syu8384

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
